campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to accept a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth pay for recreation programs. The council also discussed extending a temporary local law and approved moving forward with plans to build a safer bicycle and pedestrian crossing over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth recreation scholarships
Extension of interim ordinance #2326
Hamilton Avenue bike/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17
Grant application for highway crossing project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could speak about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city rule) and skipping the usual first-reading formality to speed up the process. The council then scheduled the ordinance to be voted on and officially adopted at their next regular meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on a proposed ordinance
First reading waived to expedite the ordinance adoption process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next council meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to finalize their 2026 weed control efforts and approve new operating rules for council meetings. They also addressed affordable housing policies, approved raising the Progress Pride and Juneteenth flags, heard from the local transit authority, and set their calendar for implementing new housing plans.
keyTopics: Completed 2026 weed abatement program results
New rules for how City Council conducts meetings
Affordable housing unit transfer policy changes
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raising approvals
Housing element implementation timeline for coming year

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several contracts and agreements to move forward with construction projects and professional services. The council also voted to join a statewide insurance program to help manage the town's insurance costs and risks.
keyTopics: Construction contract with QLM, Inc. for a town project
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority for better insurance rates
Professional services agreement with Dudek (likely engineering or planning firm)
Updates to existing agreements with NHA and other service providers
Art transfer agreement (purpose not fully detailed in agenda)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review how well the city is supporting kids and teens through various programs and services. They discussed updates on youth programs like the Youth Empowerment Alliance and Safe Summer Initiative, checked on code enforcement efforts, and reviewed plans to improve library facilities and services.
keyTopics: Status of the Children and Youth Services Master Plan
Youth Empowerment Alliance and Safe Summer Initiative Grant programs
Code Enforcement Annual Report findings
Library facility improvements and customer experience updates

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate both in person and online. Residents could join the virtual meeting through Zoom or phone to listen and speak during public comment periods.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
How to raise hand or use *9 to request speaking time
How to unmute via *6 if calling by phone

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the meeting minutes from their previous gathering in March.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by three Human Relations Commissioners
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Human Relations Commission regular business

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community initiatives including a presentation about Budget and Bites (likely a food/nutrition program), hear from a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and announce summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved meeting minutes from the previous month and opened the floor for residents to speak about any local issues.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City of Cupertino summer job opportunities
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using both in-person and online participation options. Residents can attend remotely via Zoom or phone, and submit written comments through the city's online system before the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Zoom meeting details provided (ID: 997-0675-9306)
Phone dial-in option for remote participation
Public comment submission through eComments system

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve several administrative and financial matters, including joining a tax board partnership with the county, potentially waiving fees for a community educational organization, and moving forward with a construction project. The council also reviewed updates to the city's 5-year community development plan and considered new work requests from individual council members.
keyTopics: Joining county tax board partnership
Fee waiver and donation decision for Milpitas Community Educational
Construction project approval and contract award
Updates to 2025-2030 community development plan
New council member work requests

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss the city's natural gas utility finances and rates for the next fiscal year. The main decision involved approving a financial plan for the gas utility and adjusting rates for residential and commercial customers, with the meeting focusing on this one-hour agenda item.
keyTopics: Natural gas utility budget and financial forecast for 2027
Gas rate changes for homes and businesses
Approval of reserve fund transfers and general fund transfers
Modifications to three different gas rate schedules (residential, master-metered, and large commercial)
